fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Minnesota Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Minnesota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity minnesota, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to minnesota, respiratory therapist compact minnesota, minnesota respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Minnesota Board of Medical Practice — RT
board.mailing_address: Minnesota Board of Medical Practice, University Park Plaza, 2829 University Ave. S.E., Suite 500, Minneapolis, MN 55414-3246

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Minnesota.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Register for an account and complete the Respiratory Therapist Initial Application via the **Board's Online Services Portal** at https://bbp.hlb.state.mn.us/.

steps.title: Arrange for Official Transcripts and NBRC Verification
steps.description: Request your official academic transcripts be sent directly from your educational institution and your NBRC credential verification (CRT and/or RRT) be sent directly from the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) to the Minnesota Board of Medical Practice.

steps.title: Complete Verification of Supervised Clinical Practice
steps.description: If you are applying for licensure after January 1, 2017, ensure your program director completes and submits the 'Verification of Supervised Clinical Practice for Respiratory Therapist Licensure' form, confirming completion of 1500 hours of supervised clinical practice.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: After submitting your online application and paying the required fee, await email instructions from the Board regarding the fingerprinting process for the mandatory Criminal Background Check (CBC).

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable initial application fee and Criminal Background Check fee through the **Board's Online Services Portal** during the application submission process.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Minnesota respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Minnesota have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Minnesota. To work in Minnesota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Minnesota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Minnesota
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Minnesota, applicants must create an account and submit their application online through the Board's Online Services Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: To start your application, navigate to the Board's Online Services Portal. Click 'Register Now' to create a new user account using your email address and a password. Once registered, log in to the portal and select the 'Initial Application' for Respiratory Therapists to begin the application process.
